name: Valeriy Grachev
title: Platform Engineering Lead
meta_description: Valeriy Grachev — Platform Engineering Lead. Regulated crypto trading infrastructure: multi-region AWS, Kubernetes, Internal Developer Platform for institutional OTC, market-making & arbitrage. $170B+ annual volume. Zero major incidents.
og_description: Platform Engineering Lead for regulated crypto trading infrastructure. $170B+ annual volume, 5 AWS regions, 50% cloud cost reduction. Zero major incidents in 2+ years.
contact_note:
Open to platform engineering leadership roles,
consulting, and advisory for crypto/fintech
infrastructure. Remote preferred.
about: I build and lead the platform engineering function for a regulated crypto trading company — institutional OTC, market-making, and arbitrage systems processing $170B+ annually across the network. Zero major incidents in 2+ years, 50% cloud cost reduction through FinOps practices, and an internal developer platform that lets engineering teams ship independently. Previously co-founded a B2B marketplace serving 30K+ customers, and volunteered as DevOps Lead for helpingtoleave.org — Ukraine’s largest civilian evacuation effort (92K+ people assisted).

bullets: Own the entire platform engineering function for FinchTrade and Finery Markets (CNBC Top 300 Fintech) — a Swiss OTC desk and regulated crypto ECN. Report to CEO; own infrastructure, security, compliance, and developer platform serving 15+ engineers across trading, market-making, and arbitrage teams. 5 AWS regions, zero maintenance windows

Architected multi-region AWS platform (5 regions, active-active) with domain-isolated EKS clusters enforcing blast-radius boundaries between trading core, OTC, and compliance workloads. 400+ pods, 50+ microservices, zero major incidents in 2+ years across 24/7 markets with no maintenance windows

Built internal developer platform reducing service onboarding from days to minutes: developers write a 15-line deploy.yaml and get complete CI/CD with IRSA, secrets management, and database provisioning for 145+ repos. Evaluated Backstage; built purpose-fit tooling (FastAPI + React) due to crypto-domain regulatory constraints
Achieved 50% cloud cost reduction: Karpenter with Graviton/ARM spot instances, Kubecost for per-team spend attribution, and custom Slack cost bot for real-time anomaly alerts

Led organization through SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 audits with zero critical findings. MiCA readiness for EU crypto regulation. Nitro Enclaves for custody-adjacent confidential computing, container image signing (cosign/Sigstore), Kyverno blocking non-compliant deployments

Built and operated infrastructure for one of Ukraine’s largest civilian evacuation organizations — 92K+ people assisted, 16K+ fully guided evacuations from frontline and occupied territories. Featured by Harvard Ukrainian Research Institute
Led team of 5 DevOps engineers and coordinated with 30+ tech specialists. Core platform: Telegram bot network serving 300+ volunteers coordinating real-time evacuations across active war zones
AWS/EKS/CDK infrastructure with full observability stack. Mitigated state-level DDoS attacks using AWS Shield and WAF to keep evacuation services online during active military operations

Co-founded B2B marketplace platform connecting retail stores with service contractors in Kazakhstan. Built from zero to 30K+ customers with high retention rate and 1.3K+ active contractors across 254 service types
Owned all technical and product decisions: architecture, infrastructure (Kubernetes on GCP), hiring, delivery, and contractor payments. Led team of 10
Integrated with major retail chains: Alser, Mechta, Leroy Merlin, Sulpak, DNS, Technograd — enabling in-store service sales at checkout

Built enterprise education platform for Russia’s largest startup accelerator (IIDF — 400+ portfolio companies, 6B RUB fund). 30K+ students completed programs through the platform. Led team of 7. Part-time contract alongside Fevlake and AIDU roles
Built Kubernetes-based infrastructure from scratch for a DevOps consulting firm. Designed and executed monolith-to-microservices migration

projects.name: Multi-Region Fintech Platform
projects.description: Domain-isolated EKS clusters (trading core, OTC, market guard) instead of namespaces — hard blast-radius boundaries for crypto trading. Infrastructure as Software approach: Pulumi (Python) enabling unit-tested, type-safe infrastructure. Karpenter with Graviton/ARM and spot instances for sub-60s node provisioning during market volatility spikes.

projects.name: Internal Developer Platform
projects.description: Platform-as-a-Product approach: evaluated Backstage, built purpose-fit tooling due to crypto-domain regulatory constraints. ft-toolbox (FastAPI + React) with Claude API for AI-augmented operations — automated Jira digests, cost anomaly narration, Grafana audit that caught disabled SSL on 12/14 Postgres datasources. Golden paths for developer self-service.

projects.name: FinOps & Cost Engineering
projects.description: Achieved 50% cloud cost reduction across 5 AWS regions. Karpenter with Graviton/ARM and spot instances for compute, Kubecost for per-team spend attribution and anomaly detection. Built custom Slack cost bot — real-time alerts on spend spikes, weekly team-level reports, untagged resource tracking.

projects.name: Observability & Incident Intelligence
projects.description: VictoriaMetrics over Thanos — simpler ops, better compression for long-term retention. OTEL dual pipeline: metrics via Prometheus Remote Write, traces via Tempo. Robusta for AI-powered alert enrichment and auto-remediation playbooks. Every alert links to a runbook — contributing to zero major incidents in 2+ years.

projects.name: Istio Service Mesh Implementation
projects.description: Istio over Linkerd — more expressive AuthorizationPolicy for crypto compliance rules like “only compliance service calls KYT endpoint.” Validated with 17-service staging environment replicating production topology. Now rolling out to production: PERMISSIVE → STRICT mTLS per namespace.

projects.name: Security & Compliance Engineering
projects.description: Led organization through SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 audits with zero critical findings. SOPS over Vault — encrypted secrets in git, auditable, no extra infra at our scale. Container image signing (cosign/Sigstore), SBOM generation via Trivy, Nitro Enclaves for custody-adjacent confidential computing. MiCA readiness for EU crypto regulation deadline July 2026.

notifications.text: kubecost: cloud spend -3.2% vs last week
karpenter: spot interruption eu-west-1 — node replaced 47s
istio mTLS: 100% strict across all namespaces
cosign: all 52 images verified — signatures valid
